description: Trigger discrimination for the compliance cluster: the certifiable AIMS vs per-feature AI review, the voluntary RMF program, the security ISMS, the SoA, the SDLC operating model, and gap assessment.

cases.prompt: Build our ISO 42001 management system — AI policy, AI risk assessments, AI system impact assessments, internal audit, the works.
cases.reason: The certifiable AIMS with clauses 4–10 and the 6.1.2/6.1.3/6.1.4 machinery is the 42001 projection; feature reviews and the RMF method are inputs it composes.

cases.prompt: Assess the risk tier and required human oversight for our AI support-ticket auto-responder before it ships.
cases.reason: One AI feature's tiering/oversight/disclosure go/no-go is the Phase 7 reviewer; the AIMS is the org-level system aggregating such reviews.

cases.prompt: Adopt the NIST AI RMF — set up GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, and MANAGE across how we build and run AI.
cases.reason: Operationalizing the voluntary RMF functions is the lifecycle manager; the AIMS is the certifiable wrapper that can sit on top of it.

cases.prompt: Design our information security management system for certification — risk register, SoA, internal audits.
cases.reason: The information-security ISMS is 27001's projection; the AIMS governs AI systems specifically, sharing the harmonized skeleton.

cases.prompt: Write the 42001 Statement of Applicability from our AI risk treatment decisions.
cases.reason: The SoA document serves both ISO standards from its own skill; the AIMS architect designs the treatment process feeding it.

cases.prompt: Define how humans and coding agents plan, implement, review, and merge work together — stages, gates, and authority.
cases.reason: The human+agent SDLC contract is the Phase 1.5 operating model; the AIMS maps it as an operational mechanism inside clause 8.
